vulnerabilities: Although access controls are enforced at the site perimeter and/or building entrance(s), the facility has limited or no internal access controls.
Although background checks are conducted on contact security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
Although background checks are conducted on contactors personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
Although background checks are conducted on vendors, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
Although the facility conducts background checks on employee security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
Although the facility conducts background checks on employees, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
Although the facility has a badging system in place, it is not strictly enforced.
Background checks are not conducted on contract security personnel.
Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
Background checks are not conducted on vendors.
Building Envelope
CCTV coverage of areas of concern, such as gates and entryways, is limited.
CCTV coverage of the facility's perimeter is limited.
CCTV coverage of the main vehicle entrance area is limited or non-existent.
Electronic Security Systems
Entry Controls
Facility Information
Facility personnel are not aware of the nationwide

Facility personnel are not aware of the nationwide 'If You See Something, Say Something' public awareness campaign. This program is intended to raise public awareness of indicators of terrorism and terrorism-related crime and to emphasize the importance of reporting suspicious activity to the proper authorities.

Facility personnel have unrestricted access throughout the site. Controls are not in place to restrict employee access to sensitive/critical areas (e.g., control rooms, server rooms, fuel or chemical storage areas, utility service areas) to only those with a valid need for admittance.
Fences, gates, and/or parking areas are not illuminated. A lack of quality lighting can reduce observation capabilities and diminish the safety and security posture of the facility.
First Preventers-Responders
Ground-floor windows do not have protective measures to mitigate the hazardous effects of flying glass during an explosive event.
Illumination
Information Sharing
Less than 100% of the facility is enclosed. The lack of fencing may allow unrestricted access to the facility.

Lighting for fences, gates, and/or parking areas is uneven and dissimilar, causing glare and shadows, and resulting in inconsistent coverage, which can produce dark areas and shadows where intruders can go undetected.
Main entrance doors are not locked, or existing locks are ineffective.
One or more unobstructed high-speed avenues of approach lead to significant areas and critical assets of the facility.
Outside air intakes are accessible and susceptible to the introduction of CBR agents.
Parking in the uncontrolled parking area is not monitored in any way.
Parking, Delivery, Standoff
Perimeter Security
Resilience Management
Resilience Management - Business Continuity
Resilience Management - Emergency Action Plan
Security Force
Security Management
The backup power supply covers emergency lighting for only critical locations along fences and/or gates and/or in parking areas. The backup power supply does not cover most of the existing lights.
The CCTV video is not recorded; therefore, the facility cannot review footage to investigate or evaluate security incidents.
The corporate-level security plan does not reflect facility-specific needs.
The customers, employees or volunteers do not display or practice situational awareness related to security.
The facility does not conduct any background checks.
The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
The facility does not conduct background checks on employee security personnel.
The facility does not exchange information through the HSIN portal. Using the HSIN portal for information sharing would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information via InfraGard. Becoming a member of InfraGard would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with any federal agencies. Information exchange with federal agencies would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with local law enforcement. Information exchange with local law enforcement would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with state and/or major urban area fusion centers. Information exchange fusion centers would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with state law enforcement. Information exchange with state law enforcement would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with the FBI. Information exchange with the FBI would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exchange information with the ISAC for its sector. Information exchange with the ISAC would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.

The facility does not exchange information with the local emergency management agency. Information exchange with the local emergency management agency would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.

The facility does not exchange information with the state emergency management agency. Information exchange with the state emergency management agency would enhance the security and resilience posture of the facility.
The facility does not exercise its business continuity plan annually.
The facility does not exercise its emergency operation/emergency action plan annually.
The facility does not exercise its security plan annually.
The facility does not have a badging system.

The facility does not have a CCTV system. CCTV systems provide a rapid and cost-effective method for determining the source of intrusion or other detection alarms, if they are in place, and support surveillance of activities and events within and around a facility. In addition, a well-designed CCTV system provides a cost-effective supplement to security force patrols.
The facility does not have a CCTV system. CCTV systems support surveillance of activities and events within and around a facility.

The facility does not have a designated business continuity manager. The lack of a designated business continuity manager may hinder the implementation of business continuity policies, programs, or procedures.
The facility does not have a designated security manager. The lack of a security manager may hinder the application of security policies, programs, or procedures.

The facility does not have a mass notification system or process.  During an event, no method exists to alert all participants of current threats and the appropriate responses.  The lack of communication or notification may lead to increased injuries or deaths.
The facility does not have a perimeter fence. The lack of perimeter fencing may allow unrestricted access to the facility and critical areas within the facility.

The facility does not have a security force. Employees perform security-related duties such as patrols and security checks. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.

The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
The facility does not have a surge capacity plan to provide additional security personnel during special circumstances or elevated threats.
The facility does not have a vehicle barrier plan or system to mitigate high speed vehicle intrusion or address heavy traffic flow during special events
The facility does not have a written business continuity plan.
The facility does not have a written emergency operation/emergency action plan.
The facility does not have a written security plan.

The facility does not have a written security plan. Although the facility may have documentation that addresses security policies, programs, and/or procedures, it does not have a separate, comprehensive security plan.
The facility does not have an alternate site for continuity of business when the primary location is not accessible.
The facility does not have an interior IDS to detect attempted and successful security breaches.

The facility does not have an onsite security force. The company/organization employs a security force, but security personnel are not stationed at the facility. This limits the facility's ability to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies as they occur at the facility.

The facility does not have an onsite security force. The company/organization employs a security force, but security personnel are not stationed at the facility. This limits the facility's ability to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies as they occur at the facility.

The facility does not have comprehensive post orders for the security force, which may result in situations where security personnel do not fulfill their duties and/or perform outside the scope of their duties..

The facility does not have interoperable communications with the primary emergency medical response agency. Without interoperable communications, the facility cannot communicate with first responders in order to coordinate response activities.

The facility does not have interoperable communications with the primary fire response agency. Without interoperable communications, the facility cannot communicate with first responders in order to coordinate response activities.

The facility does not have interoperable communications with the primary law enforcement agency. Without interoperable communications, the facility cannot communicate with first responders in order to coordinate security and response activities.
The facility does not participate in any security working groups.
The facility does not train personnel on the business continuity plan.
The facility does not train personnel on the emergency operation/emergency action plan.
The facility does not train personnel on the security plan
The facility does not train volunteers, ushers, and members of the congregation or organization on emergency operation or emergency response.
The facility does not use a proximity card access system.
The facility does not use barriers to mitigate high-speed avenues of approach. Barriers would reduce vehicle speeds and/or prevent vehicle penetration.
The facility does not use real-time monitoring for the CCTV system, which limits the facility to reviewing recorded material only.
The facility has limited or no access control policies/procedures for contractors/vendors.
The facility has limited or no access control policies/procedures for customers/patrons/public.
The facility has limited or no access control policies/procedures for employees.
The facility has limited or no access control policies/procedures for visitors.
The facility has limited or no access control procedures/policies for special events.
The facility has limited or no vehicle access control policies/procedures.

The facility has no security team or threat assessment team to identify potential threats or respond to security incidents.  The lack of a security team may allow potential threats to go unnoticed and leave the facility without the ability to respond to security incidents.

The facility has no security team or threat assessment team to identify potential threats or respond to security incidents.  The lack of a security team may allow potential threats to go unnoticed and leave the facility without the ability to respond to security incidents.
The facility has not coordinated the emergency operation/emergency action plan with emergency responders.
The facility has not coordinated the security plan with local law enforcement.

The facility has not designated or identified safe rooms for use during events where shelter-in-place, hide, or lockdown are appropriate.  The use of the safe room may be in response to an active-shooter event.
The facility has not tested, exercised or documented its mass notification system.  Using an untested system can cause confusion when used during an incident.
The facility has not tested, exercised or documented its mass notification system.  Using an untested system can cause confusion when used during an incident.
The facility has numerous extraneous access points for ingress, or has numerous access points that are unsecured.
The facility lacks key control policies and procedures. Without controls, keys may be easily misused or duplicated without authorization, and access control to the facility may be compromised.
The facility lacks keycard (e.g., proximity card, swipe card) control policies and procedures. Without controls, keycards may be easily misused, and access control to the facility may be compromised.
The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
The facility trains some, but not all, personnel on the business continuity plan.
The facility's security plan is missing key elements.
The fence is not clearly marked or identified with warning signs.
The main entrance into the building allows for unrestricted access into the facility.

The primary emergency medical response agency has not conducted an onsite visit of the facility. Onsite visits can familiarize first responders with the facility, key personnel, site layout, and other issues that would enhance incident response.

The primary fire response agency has not conducted an onsite visit of the facility. Onsite visits can familiarize first responders with the facility, key personnel, site layout, and other issues that would enhance incident response.

The primary law enforcement agency has not conducted an onsite visit of the facility. Onsite visits can familiarize first responders with the facility, key personnel, site layout, and other issues that would enhance incident response.
The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
The security force does not receive training on CPR/First Aid.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures for active shooter attacks.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures for bomb threats.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures for break-ins.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures for fires.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures for hostage/barricade situations.
The security force does not receive training on emergency response procedures in the event of a HAZMAT release.
The security force does not receive training on IED recognition.
The security force does not receive training on procedures in the event of a natural disaster.
The security force does not receive training on procedures in the event of a natural disaster.
The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
There are no procedures or policies to identify and act on unauthorized vehicles parked in the uncontrolled parking area for an extended period.
There is a lack of formal, consistent sharing of security and safety information with neighboring properties, adjacent tenants, or associated departments.
There is no capability or process for an employee to report a security concern.
Uncontrolled parking is available above the building.
Uncontrolled parking is available adjacent to the facility, on the street.
Uncontrolled parking is available under the building.

Vehicles can park within 400 feet of the facility. Access to the parking area is uncontrolled. The lack of rigorous vehicle access control procedures increases the vulnerability of the facility to a vehicle-borne attack.
